title: Arrabidaea fasciculata
taxonomy: Arrabidaea fasciculata belongs to the family Bignonaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as clockvine or grapebine. The genus Arrabidaea is a part of this family and is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. Arrabidaea fasciculata is classified as a species of flowering plant within the genus Arrabidaea and is characterized by its woody vines and clusters of small flowers. The specific classification of Arrabidaea fasciculata includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Lamiales, family Bignonaceae, genus Arrabidaea, and species A. fasciculata.
